title: 2 Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
body_text:
2	Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions
2.1	Definitions
2-Step Security
2-step security is a type of step-up authentication thatâs added to an application. To log in to an application thatâs protected by 2-step security, a user must enter the one-time password from their registered 2-step security token.
2-Step Security Token
A 2-step security token is a device or application that generates a random one-time password thatâs recognized by applications that are protected by 2-step security.
ABA Number
The ABA Number is a unique code used to identify financial institutions in the US. Its function is like a Transit Number.
Account Holder
An Account Holder is an individual or entity that has an account at your financial institution.
AML
Anti-Money Laundering (AML) is a term used to describe the legal controls that require financial institutions and other regulated entities to prevent or report money laundering activities.
Bank to Bank Wire
A Bank to Bank Wire is a wire your financial institution creates in which another financial institution is the beneficiary.
BCFSA
The BC Financial Services Authority, formerly The Financial Institutions Commission (FICOM), is a crown regulatory agency of the British Columbia Government.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications, and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage User Management Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CSIS
The Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) plays a leading role in protecting the national security interests of Canada by investigating and reporting on threats to the security of Canada.
DGCM
The Deposit Guarantee Corporation of Manitoba (DGCM) monitors and analyzes the lending practices, financial performance, and operating procedures of each credit union in Manitoba.
Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) System
The Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system provides alerts for potentially fraudulent transactions for supported payment products. EFM has fraud monitoring, detection, and prevention capabilities, including the use of fraud models, machine learning, artificial intelligence, and rules. Your financial institution uses the alerts generated in EFM to investigate suspected fraud and to report the results in EFM.
Exotic Currency
An exotic currency is a foreign exchange term for a thinly traded currency.
FINTRAC
The Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC) receives, analyzes, assesses, and discloses financial intelligence on suspected money laundering, terrorist financing, and threats to the security of Canada.
Foreign Funds
Foreign Funds are any currencies that arenât Canadian.
FSRA
The Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) which assumed the regulatory functions of the Financial Services Commission of Ontario (FSCO), regulates insurance, pensions, credit unions,
caisses populaires
, cooperatives, mortgage brokers, and loan and trust companies in Ontario.
Home Transit Number
The home transit number is the primary transit number associated with an MTS Administrator or a Wires user.
IBAN
The International Bank Account Number (IBAN) was introduced in Europe as a standard means of recognizing and validating account numbers in cross-border electronic payments. The IBAN contains the beneficiaryâs basic account number, the country code of the destination country, and IBAN Check Digits for validation purposes.
IBAN Check Digits
IBAN check digits are a calculated field used to identify errors in the IBAN. An invalid check digit indicates that one or more characters in the IBAN are invalid. To determine which characters are invalid, review the memberâs source documents. See Figure 1 to identify the IBAN Check Digits.
Figure 1: IBAN Format
Incoming Wire
An incoming wire is a wire another financial institution creates which credits your financial institution, an account holder of your financial institution, or a non-account holder.
ISO 20022
An electronic messaging standard used for exchanging data rich payment information between financial institutions.
Large Cash Transaction
A large cash transaction is cash received in the amount of $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more from an individual in the course of a single transaction. This includes aggregate transactions in which two or more transactions totalling $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more are made within 24 hours, where the financial entity knows that the transactions are conducted by or on behalf of the same person.
Large International Wire
A large international incoming wire is an incoming wire of $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more that originated from outside Canada.
Level 1 Authorization Limit
The Level 1 Authorization limit is a branch setting in MTS that designates the dollar amount at which outgoing wires created in MDB or Forge Commercial require approval in the Wires application.
Note:	The Level 1 Authorization limit isnât used for in-branch wires. All in-branch wires require at least one approval.
Level 2 Authorization Limit
The Level 2 Authorization limit is a branch setting in MTS that designates the dollar amount at which an outgoing wire requires two approvals in the Wires application. Amounts greater than the Level 2 Authorization limit require two approvals.
Live Rates
Live Rates are foreign exchange rates (including your foreign exchange spread, if applicable) used in Wires in place of static rates. A snapshot of live rates can be viewed via the View Rates option in Wires.
Lynx
Lynx is Canadaâs electronic high-value payment system that participating financial institutions use to send Canadian dollar wire payments on behalf of their customers. Wires cleared and settled using Lynx are fast, irrevocable, and made with real-time settlement finality. Lynx supports the ISO 20022 financial messaging standard, allowing rich remittance data such as invoice details or purchase information to travel with a wire.
Major Currency
A major currency is a foreign currency thatâs frequently bought and sold.
Minor Currency
A minor currency is a foreign currency thatâs less frequently bought and sold.
MTS
The Money Transfer System (MTS) is used to manage Wires Users, MTS Administrators, and branch information for the Wires application.
Non-account Holder
A non-account holder is an individual or entity that doesnât have an account at your financial institution.
Outgoing Wire
An outgoing wire is a wire your financial institution creates in which another financial institution, a client of that financial institution, or a non-client is the beneficiary.
PCMLTFA
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
(
PCMLTFA
) is an Act to facilitate combatting the laundering of proceeds of crime and the financing of terrorist activities.
PCMLTF Regulations
The regulations passed under
PCMLTFA
, include the following:
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Suspicious Transaction Reporting Regulations
Cross-border Currency and Monetary Instruments Reporting Regulations
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Administrative Monetary Penalties Regulations
Pegged Currency
A pegged currency is a country or governmentâs exchange rate policy whereby it attaches, or links, the central bankâs rate of exchange to another countryâs script.
PEP
A Politically Exposed Person, meaning a politically exposed foreign person, a politically exposed domestic person, or head of an international organization, as defined in
PCMLTFA
, or a prescribed family member or close associate of such person.
Politically Exposed Domestic Person means a person who holds, or has held within the last five years, one of the following offices or positions:
â
Governor General, lieutenant governor, or head of government;
â
member of the Senate or House of Commons or member of a legislature;
â
deputy minister or equivalent rank;
â
ambassador, or attachÃ© or counsellor of an ambassador;
â
military officer with a rank of general or above;
â
president of a corporation thatâs wholly owned directly by His Majesty in right of Canada or a province;
â
head of a government agency;
â
judge of an appellate court in a province, the Federal Court of Appeal, or the Supreme Court of Canada;
â
leader or president of a political party represented in a legislature; or
â
the office or position of mayor or the head of a city, town, village, or municipality.
Politically Exposed Foreign Person means a person who holds or has held one of the following offices or positions in or on behalf of a foreign state:
â
head of state or head of government;
â
member of the executive council of government or member of a legislature;
â
deputy minister or equivalent rank;
â
ambassador or attachÃ© or counsellor of an ambassador;
â
military officer with a rank of general or above;
â
president of a state-owned company or a state-owned bank;
â
head of a government agency;
â
judge of a supreme court, constitutional court, or other court of last resort; or
â
leader or president of a political party represented in a foreign legislature.
Head of an International Organization (HIO) means a person who holds, or has held within the past five years, the office or position of head of an international organization thatâs established by the governments of more than one country by means of a formally signed agreement between the governments of more than one country.
Family member means, in respect of a foreign politically exposed person, domestic politically exposed person, or head of an international organization, the individualâs:
â
biological or adoptive child;
â
parent;
â
parentâs child;
â
spouse or common-law partner;
â
ex-spouse or common-law partner (with respect to a Foreign PEP only);
â
ex-spouse or common-law partner (with respect to a current Domestic PEP or HIO only); or
â
spouseâs or common-law partnerâs parent.
Close associate means a person closely connected to a senior official or an HIO for personal (social) or business (professional) reasons.
Sender
A sender is an account holder or non-account holder who requests an outgoing wire.
Sending Branch
The sending branch is the branch thatâs debited for an outgoing wire.
ServiceNow
ServiceNow is the web-based ticketing application used to report service issues, request changes to existing services, or submit questions to Central 1.
Step-up Authentication
Step-up authentication is any additional identity verification (e.g., a 2-step security token) required to validate a userâs access to a higher security level.
SWIFT
The Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication (SWIFT) is an international cooperative that provides standardized wire messaging and software services for financial institutions. Wires is SWIFT compliant.
Third Party
A third party is a person or entity who instructs the person or entity carrying out an activity or transaction to do so on their behalf.
Transit Number
A transit number is the unique code used to identify financial institutions in Canada. For Wires purposes, the transit number consists of a leading zero, the 3-digit institution number, and the 5-digit branch number (e.g., 080912010, 082812010).
User Management
The User Management application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It's also used to edit and delete other User Management Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens.
User Management Security Officers
User Management Security Officers use the User Management  application to add, modify, and delete Client Centre Users and to modify and delete other User Management Security Officers. User Management Security Officers are also referred to as Security Officers. User Management Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications
Very Large International Incoming Wire
A very large international incoming wire is an incoming wire of $100,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more that originated from outside Canada.
Very Large International Outgoing Wires
A very large international outgoing wire is an outgoing wire of $100,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more thatâs sent outside Canada.
Wires
Wires is an application on the
PaymentStream
Directâ¢ platform that allows authorized users to create and receive domestic and international wires.
2.2	Frequently Asked Questions
User Management
Question
The user I created canât access Wires. Why?
Answer
There are several reasons why a user may not be able to log in to MTS. Verify the following:
The user was set up on both MTS and User Management and all changes were authorized.
The userâs Client Centre and MTS profiles contain the same user ID and transit number.
The userâs Client Centre ID was assigned access to the PSD-Wires application.
The user isnât locked on User Management or suspended in MTS.
See Chapter 8, Wires User Management.
Wire Fees
Question
If we customized Wires to pre-populate fees, can we change or waive fees by wire?
Answer
Yes. If your financial institution has customized Wires to pre-populate your customer fees, users can override the pre-populated value.
Question
Does Central 1 add the wire processing fee to the outgoing wire fee that we customize on Wires?
Answer
The Central 1 wire processing fee is charged to your Central 1 account at month end and isnât automatically included in the fee that you charge your customer. The fee amount that displays in Wires is what you are charging your customer. To recover your processing costs, ensure that the fee you charge your customer (pre-populated or manually entered) is at minimum the value of the Central 1 processing fee.
See Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees.
Limits
Question
How are user MTS User limits applied in Wires?
Answer
In Wires, user limits apply to wire approvals. There are no limits to create wires.
The Transfer Limit
â The maximum amount of a single outgoing wire that you can approve in Wires.
The Daily Limit
â The maximum amount of all outgoing wires that you can approve in a business day.
Wire limits are set in Canadian dollars.
All wires require at least one approval:
If the value of the wire is less than or equal to the branch MTS Level 2 Authorization limit, one approval is required.
If the value of the wire is greater than the branch MTS Level 2 Authorization limit, two approvals are required.
See Section 9.5, Entering Branch Settings in MTS.
Question
The amount of the foreign currency wire I am trying to approve is less than my transfer limit. Why wonât the system allow me to approve the wire?
Answer
Transfer limits are set in Canadian dollars. Foreign currency wires are converted to Canadian dollars using a live rate and are compared to your transfer limits set in MTS. If the Canadian equivalent amount exceeds your transfer limit you canât approve the wire. This conversion is only for authorization and doesnât affect the currency of the outgoing wire. See Section 8.6, Creating a Wires User in MTS.
Note: Rates arenât live on Saturdays, they are held from Friday through to Sunday.
Routing Instructions
Question
Our customer needs to provide the sender with our financial institutionâs routing instructions. How do I find out what our routing instructions are?
Answer
The routing instructions for the most common currency types are published as forms. You can complete these forms and provide them to your customers for further distribution to the senders. Routing instruction forms are available as follows:
Routing Instructions â Canadian Dollar (CAD) Domestic Incoming Wires, Form 2605
Routing Instructions â US Dollar (USD) Incoming Wires, Form 2606
Routing Instructions â Canadian Dollar (CAD) International Incoming Wires, Form 2607
Routing Instructions â Euro (EUR) Incoming Wires, Form 3710.
Routing Instructions â Australian Dollar (AUD) Incoming Wires, Form 3771
Routing Instructions â Swiss Franc (CHF) Incoming Wires, Form 3772
Routing Instructions â British Pound Sterling (GBP) Incoming Wires, Form 3773
Routing Instructions â Japanese Yen (JPY) Incoming Wires, Form 3774
Routing Instructions â Mexican Peso (MXN) Incoming Wires, Form 3775
Routing Instructions â Polish Zloty (PLN) Incoming Wires, Form 3776
Routing Instructions â New Zealand Dollar (NZD) Incoming Wires, Form 9352
See Section 24.2, Routing Instructions for Incoming Wires.
Creating Outgoing
Wires
Question
What should I do if I canât find the beneficiaryâs financial institution in Wires?
Answer
If you canât locate the financial institution on Wires, contact Central 1 for assistance.
Question
I created an outgoing wire and had another user authorize it. Why is it still unauthorized?
Answer
If the amount of the outgoing wire exceeds the MTS Level 2 Authorization Limit, then two users must approve it. More information on authorization limits is provided above. See Limits, Question 2. Also, see Chapter 22, Approving Outgoing Wires.
END
